title: Monopolistic competition
description: Practice what you've learned about monopolistic competition in this exercise.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Advertising is most likely to be used in which kind of market?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: monopolistic competition
perfect competition
oligopoly
monopoly
perfect price discriminating monopoly

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements correctly identifies a difference between monopoly and monopolistic competition?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There are barriers to entry in monopoly, but not in monopolistic competition.
The demand curve is downward sloping in monopoly, but not in monopolistic competition.
There is only one firm in monopoly, but there are a few firms in monopolistic competition.
There are many buyers in monopoly, but there are only a few buyers in monopolistic competition.
The monopoly firm charges a markup, but a monopolistically competitive firm does not.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements correctly identifies a difference between perfect competition and monopolistic competition?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: In perfect competition, goods are identical, but goods are slightly differentiated in monopolistic competition.
In perfect competition, there are many buyers and many sellers, but in monopolistic competition there are few buyers and few sellers.
In perfect competition, there are barriers to entry, but there are no barriers to entry in monopolistic competition.
In perfect competition, firms markup the price of their good, but in monopolistic competition firms cannot markup the price of their good.
In perfect competition, firms cannot earn positive economic profits in the long run, but in monopolistic competition firms can earn positive economic profits in the long run.

assessment_items.question_content:
**All of these options describe a characteristic of monopolistic competition EXCEPT which option?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: identical products
no barriers to entry
many buyers
many sellers
a downward-sloping demand curve

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of these graphs correctly illustrates a monopolistic competition in long-run equilibrium?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: ![A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. There are five curves and lines displayed. The downward-sloping line labeled D represents demand. Another downward-sloping line labeled M R represents marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts low, below the D-line, and increases steeply as it moves to the right. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D-line, decreases to a minimum point near Q 1, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. Curve A T C and line D intersect at (Q 1, P 4). Curve M C and line M R intersect at (Q 1, P 1).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/c1e01b44d06977dd09aafc447b49259bfb4c7c83)

![A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. There are five curves and lines displayed. The downward-sloping line labeled D represents demand. Another downward-sloping line labeled M R represents marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts low, below the D-line, and increases steeply as it moves to the right. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D-line, decreases to a minimum point near Q 1, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. Dashed lines extend horizontally from price levels P 4 and P 1 to the Y-axis and vertically from quantity Q 1 to the X-axis. Curve A T C and line D intersect at (Q 1, P 4). Curve M C and line M R intersect at (Q 1, P 1).](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/345ac55c3940f2eeb5d2bdd6e39ef329bdfd0201)

![A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. Four curves and lines are displayed. The horizontal line labeled P equals M C represents price and marginal cost equality. A downward-sloping straight line labeled D equals M R represents demand and marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts low, increases steeply as it moves to the right, and intersects the D equals M R line at quantity Q 1. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D equals M R line, decreases to a minimum point near Q 1, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. A dashed vertical line extends from quantity Q 1 on the X-axis to the intersection of M C and D equals M R, representing the equilibrium point.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/3e061f46bb228a39951da4eef4c6e6c132527411)

![A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. Four curves and lines are displayed. The horizontal line labeled P equals M C represents price and marginal cost equality. A downward-sloping straight line labeled D equals M R represents demand and marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts low, increases steeply as it moves to the right, and intersects the D equals M R line at quantity Q 1. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D equals M R line, decreases to a minimum point near Q 1, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. A dashed vertical line extends from quantity Q 1 on the X-axis to the intersection of M C and D equals M R, while a horizontal dashed line from the same point extends to the P equals M C line on the Y-axis,.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/538bc7f5f41edf54669bea2a1eac6cd760afcc2d)

![A line graph depicts the relationship between cost, price, and quantity for a firm. The graph has a vertical axis labeled Cost, in dollars, and a horizontal axis labeled Quantity. Four curves and lines are displayed. The horizontal line labeled P equals M C represents price and marginal cost equality. A downward-sloping straight line labeled D equals M R represents demand and marginal revenue. The upward-sloping curve labeled M C starts low, increases steeply as it moves to the right, and intersects both the D equals M R line and the A T C curve near quantity Q 1. The U-shaped curve labeled A T C starts above the D equals M R line, decreases to a minimum point near Q 1, and then increases to the top right. The U-shaped curve labeled A V C starts below the A T C curve, decreases slightly, and then increases, remaining below the A T C curve. Dashed lines extend horizontally from the P equals M C line and vertically from Q 1 on the X-axis to the intersection point of the curves, representing the equilibrium price and quantity.](web+graphie:${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a89fb2e1a2f76e27628a936e8815075cbcc51296)

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes what happens when firms enter an industry with monopolistic competition?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The demand curve for existing firms decreases and gets more elastic.
The demand curve for existing firms increases and becomes perfectly elastic.
The supply curve increases.
The average total cost curve decreases.
The marginal cost curve increases.

assessment_items.question_content:
Dinosaur Inn is a hotel that differentiates itself from other hotels in a market with many buyers by offering rooms with different dinosaur themes, such as the T-Rex Relax room and the Bambiraptor Suite.

**Which of the following will be true for the Dinosaur Inn when this industry is in long-run equilibrium?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Price exceeds marginal cost, there are no economic profits, and it has excess capacity.
Price is less than marginal revenue, there are positive economic profits, and there is no excess capacity.
Price exceeds marginal revenue, there are positive economic profits, and it has excess capacity.
Price is less than marginal revenue, there are negative economic profits, and there is no excess capacity.
Price equals marginal cost, there are negative economic profits, and it has excess capacity

assessment_items.question_content:
Murphy’s Muffins sells banana nut muffins in an industry that is monopolistically competitive. The industry is currently in long-run equilibrium.

**Which of the following terms describes the difference between the cost-minimizing quantity and the quantity that Murphy’s Muffins actually produces?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: excess capacity
allocative inefficiency
surplus quantity
deadweight loss
zero economic profits

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is a similarity between monopoly and monopolistic competition?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Both have excess capacity in the long run.
Both have a single firm.
Both can charge any price they wish to charge.
Both have barriers to entry.
Both have a few buyers.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which is true of a firm in monopolistic competition in the long run, but not true of perfect competition in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There is excess capacity.
There is no economic profit.
There is only one buyer.
There are identical products.
There are no barriers to entry.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following correctly describes economic profits and allocative efficiency for a firm in monopolistic competition in long-run equilibrium?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It earns zero economic profits and is allocatively inefficient.
It earns positive economic profits and is allocatively inefficient.
It earns negative economic profits and is allocatively efficient.
It earns zero economic profits and is allocatively efficient.
It earns zero economic profits and is allocatively neutral.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which types of firms are allocatively inefficient in the long run?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a single price monopoly and monopolistic competition
a perfect price discriminating monopolist and perfect competition
a perfect price-discriminating monopolist and monopolistic competition
a perfectly competitive firm and monopolistic competition
an oligopoly and a perfectly competitive firm

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following reasons best describes why monopolistically competitive firms are considered inefficient at allocating resources?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: In monopolistic competition, firms produce a quantity where marginal benefits exceed marginal costs.
In monopolistic competition, firms earn economic profits in the long run.
In monopolistic competition, firms can earn economic profits in the short run.
In monopolistic competition, each firm exhibits significant market power which creates barriers to entry.
In monopolistic competition, the firm’s marginal revenue curve is the same as its demand curve.
